chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُدْرِكُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ بَعْضَ صَلَاتِهِ وَ يُحْدِثُ الْإِمَامُ فَيُقَدِّمُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – A man attains part of his Salāt with the Imam, and the Imam breaks his ablution, so he asks him to lead

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/275)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/275)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ يَسْبِقُنِي الْإِمَامُ بِالرَّكْعَةِ فَتَكُونُ لِي وَاحِدَةٌ وَ لَهُ ثِنْتَانِ فَأَتَشَهَّدُ كُلَّمَا قَعَدْتُ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ فَإِنَّمَا التَّشَهُّدُ بَرَكَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, and Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Al Maysami, from Is’haq Bin Yazeed who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘May I be sacrificed for youasws! The Imam is ahead of me by the one Rak’at, so it happens to be one for me and for him being two. So, should I perform Tashahhud every time I sit?’ So heasws said: ‘Yes, for rather, the Tashahhud is a Blessing’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Muhammad and Muhammad ibn al-Hassan have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from al-Mithamiy from Ishaq ibn Yazid who has said the following: “I once said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, the Imam is one Rak‘at ahead of me which is the first for me and the second for him. Do I say Tashahhud (testimonies of belief) when I sit down? He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, Tashahhud (testimonies of belief) is but a blessing.’”
